What you get

Wake up to a full battery

Your charger will run on a dedicated 50-amp circuit at 40-amp constant draw. Pick your model to see real charging speed:

⚡ Charging speed calculator

37miles of range / hour
Tesla's official rate on a 50A circuit (40A output, 9.6 kW).
≈ 296 miles of range added while you sleep
🌙 Night charging schedule: you’ll charge at this full rate during your scheduled overnight hours, while your home’s big appliances are idle. A/C and charging can still run together.
Installation day

The plan, step by step

Installation at 9702 Walford Mill Lane Houston Texas 77095. Inside the garage, left side-wall close to garage door. All installations are completed in a single day.

1

Easy access, zero disruption

We only need access to your garage and the electrical panel — no access inside your home is required (unless your project includes attic/crawlspace runs or an interior panel). And the power stays ON the entire time: we never shut off electricity to your home during the installation. Bonus: if you can give us remote garage access (keypad code or smart opener), you don't even need to be home — total flexibility on scheduling.

2

Subpanel install

Your main panel has the electrical capacity for the charger but no physical space left for a new breaker — so we install a subpanel fed from your main panel, creating clean breaker space for your charger’s dedicated circuit (with room for future additions).

3

New dedicated breaker

A 50-amp breaker is installed exclusively for your charger — nothing else shares this circuit.

4

Wiring & conduit

<110 ft of ¾" EMT conduit · 110ft Ft Exterior/Attic/Garage Run — routed clean and to code.

5

Mount & connect

Your Tesla Wall Connector is mounted inside the garage, left side-wall close to garage door, between 24in and 5ft above the parking surface per NEC 625.

6

Charging schedule setup

We configure your charger with off-peak night schedules so charging happens while your home’s big appliances are idle — keeping total usage within your panel’s limits. You can still run your A/C while charging.

7

Tesla Commissioning

We energize and commission your Tesla Wall Connector, connect it to your Wi-Fi (when signal strength at the charger location allows), and upload your installation records as Tesla Certified Installers for warranty purposes.

The technical bit — made simple

How the power flows

Live diagram of your circuit: General Electric → new 60A feed breaker → new subpanel → new 50A dedicated breaker → <110 ft of ¾" EMT conduit · 110ft Ft Exterior/Attic/Garage Run → Tesla Wall Connector → Tesla Vehicle.
Capacity verified

Over capacity — managed with smart scheduling

The NEC load calculation shows your panel is over capacity with the charger added. We’ll set night charging schedules so charging runs while your home’s big loads are off.

Calculated load including your new EV charger
(NEC 220.53 / 220.82)
Subpanel load50A of 60A
! Calculated load exceeds the panel’s rating
🌙 Charging is scheduled for times when high-load appliances aren’t all running at once — heaters, water heater, dryer, oven
❄ A/C and EV charging are NOT mutually exclusive — they can run together; scheduling handles the rest
⚠ A permit is not eligible for this configuration — your Energy Advisor will walk you through it
🧰 Your panel has the capacity, but no physical breaker space — a subpanel is included to create the space for your charger’s circuit
✓ Subpanel load calculation: 50A of 60A subpanel capacity — verified within capacity

Exactly where it goes

⚠ Before installation day

  • Clear the charging location area and its surroundings — the wall and space where the charger will be mounted, whether that's in the garage, a carport, or an exterior wall. Our electrician needs safe working space.
  • Make sure we can access the electrical panel we'll be installing from — the main panel, or the subpanel if your installation uses one.
  • If the space isn't cleared on installation day, a re-schedule and additional fee may apply.

The complete Scope of Work

Every detail of your installation, documented — load calculation, line diagram, code references, and renderings.

The fine print, in plain English

Terms & conditions

Wiring & conduit — what if the location changes?
The price reflects the installation exactly as detailed in this proposal. If you change the charger's final location on-site, a $150.00 adjustment fee applies plus any additional wiring required:
1–5 ft wiring & conduit$75.00
6–10 ft wiring & conduit$150.00
11 ft and beyond$15.00 / additional ft
*Subject to availability — bigger moves may require re-scheduling.
Extended warranty options
One-year workmanship warranty is included free. Extended options:
+1 additional year$55.00
+2 additional years$105.00
+3 additional years$155.00
Just tell your Energy Advisor before installation day.
Re-scheduling & cancellations
We typically run multiple installations per day, so schedule changes affect other clients too.
Re-schedule fee$150.00
Cancellation within 24 hrs of install$200.00
Payment
Full payment is due upon installation completion. We accept check or cash to the electrician on-site, credit/debit card over the phone (3.5% processing fee), or Zelle — our account is under info@faradayelectric-ev.com.
